description: Terminal perspective — Getting Started guide for GitHub Copilot. Plan selection, IDE setup, first flight.

checkIn.title: Check-In — Choose Your Plan
checkIn.intro: Every journey starts at the check-in desk. GitHub Copilot offers five plan tiers — from a free starter to enterprise-grade with governance controls. Your plan determines which models you can access, how many premium requests you get, and what admin controls are available.

plans.name: Free
plans.cta: Good for trying Copilot before committing

plans.name: Pro
plans.audience: Individual developers who use Copilot daily
highlights: All GA models (GPT-5.x, Claude, Gemini, Grok)
plans.cta: The sweet spot for individual productivity

plans.name: Pro+
plans.audience: Power users who need frontier reasoning models constantly
plans.cta: For developers who hit Pro's premium request cap regularly

plans.name: Business
plans.audience: Teams and organizations that need admin controls
plans.cta: The standard choice for engineering teams

plans.name: Enterprise
plans.audience: Large organizations with compliance and governance requirements
plans.cta: For regulated industries and large-scale deployments

boardingPass.title: Boarding Pass — IDE Setup
boardingPass.intro: Copilot runs inside your editor — it's an extension, not a separate tool. Pick your IDE, install the extension, sign in with your GitHub account, and you're ready for departure.

ides.name: VS Code
setupSteps: Sign in with your GitHub account
ides.note: Primary IDE. All Copilot features land here first.

ides.name: JetBrains IDEs
setupSteps: Sign in with your GitHub account
ides.note: IntelliJ, PyCharm, WebStorm, Rider, GoLand, etc. All supported.

ides.name: Neovim
ides.note: Terminal-native experience. Agent Mode not yet supported.

ides.name: GitHub CLI
setupSteps: Install GitHub CLI: brew install gh
Install extension: gh extension install github/gh-copilot
ides.note: Ask Copilot questions from any terminal. Great for shell workflows.

ides.name: Xcode
setupSteps: Download 'GitHub Copilot for Xcode' from the Mac App Store
Enable in System Settings → Extensions → Xcode Source Editor
Sign in with your GitHub account
ides.note: Completions only. Chat and Agent Mode not supported in Xcode.

ides.name: Visual Studio
setupSteps: Sign in with your GitHub account
ides.note: Visual Studio 2022 17.8+. Chat available in latest versions.

firstFlight.title: First Flight — Your First Copilot Interaction
firstFlight.intro: Extension installed? Signed in? Time for your first flight. Try these three interactions — they take 60 seconds total and demonstrate the core Copilot surfaces.

exercises.title: 1. Inline Completion
exercises.instruction: Open any code file and start typing a function. Copilot suggests the rest in ghost text. Press Tab to accept.
exercises.example:
// Calculate the factorial of n
function factorial(n) {
exercises.exampleNote: After typing the function signature, pause. Copilot will suggest the body.

exercises.title: 2. Chat
exercises.instruction: Open Copilot Chat (Ctrl+Shift+I in VS Code) and ask a question about your codebase.
exercises.example: @workspace What are the main entry points in this project?
exercises.exampleNote: The @workspace participant gives Copilot context about your entire project.

exercises.title: 3. Agent Mode
exercises.instruction: In Chat, switch to Agent mode and ask Copilot to make a change. It will plan, edit files, and run terminal commands.
exercises.example: Add input validation to the login form — check email format and password length
exercises.exampleNote: Agent mode can edit multiple files and run tests. Review its plan before approving.

departures.title: Departure Board — Where to Go Next
departures.intro: You're through the terminal. Pick your next destination based on what you need:

destinations.reason: See all 48 Copilot instruments at a glance — the daily operations dashboard
X-Ray scanner for threat models, hardening steps, and compliance mapping
Deep dive into Agent Mode, Cloud Agent, MCP, and autonomous workflows
Which AI models power Copilot and how local↔cloud routing works
Admin controls, governance, sovereign cloud, and compliance frameworks
